english: lhúg-par (lhug par) śor (shor) or byuṅ (byung) Mil., tears were flow-ing abundantly. -- In some other passages the meaning of lhúg-pa (lhug pa) is not quite clear.
pf. of ltúṅ-ba (ltung ba); lhuṅ lhuṅ (lhung lhung) snyán-pai (snyan pai) sgra (sgra) sgróg-ciṅ (sgrog cing) báb-bo (bab bo) sweetly murmuring (the gentle stream) de-scends Mil.
Ssk. पिण्डपात्र, the alms-bowl of Buddha and of the mendi-cant friars.

mass, bulk, lhúṅ-can (lhung can) massy, bulky; well-fed, *lun-túg-po* (*lun tug po*) W., lhun-čé-ba (lhun che ba) very large; lhun-(gyis) grub(-pa) (lhun (gyis) grub (-pa)) acc. to Cs.: 'formed in mass, or all at once', self-created, not contrived by human labour; bgó-ba (bgo ba) daṅ (dang) bzá-ba (bza ba) lhún-gyis (lhun gyis) grúb-pas (grub pas) clothes and food coming forth of themselves Dzl.; also used of palaces, sacred buildings, images, though in such instances often only by way of compliment; lhun-grúb (lhun grub) is also noun per-sonal. -- lhún-po (lhun po), ri-rab-lhún-po (ri rab lhun po) the moun-tain of the universe, Meru, Sumeru, frq. lhun-stúg (lhun stug) Sch., lhun-túg (lhun tug) Thgy. very great, in refer-ence to the mental darkness produced by sin; prob. also: considerable, sublime, grand.

1. sbst. width, lhúb-pa-can (lhub pa can) Cs., yaṅ(s)-hlúb (yang(s) hlub), hlub-hlúb (hlub hlub) W., C. wide, of clothes. -- 2. vb.: to bind, tie, fasten, e.g. ornaments to the ear Ts., = klúb-pa (klub pa).
resp. for mṅal (mngal) the womb frq., lhúms-su (lhums su) źúgs-pai (zhugs pai) dus-mčód (dus mchod) sacri-ficial festival of the conception (of Buddha) Sch.
with lén-pa (len pa) or byéd-pa (byed pa) c. acc. to apply one's self to, bestow pains upon, = don-gnyér (don gnyer) byéd-pa (byed pa) Dzl. and elsewh.
Cs. 'filth or dross in the bowels, causing obstruction'; acc. to others: internal excrescences, v. skran (skran); Sch.: lhen (lhen) or lhen-sná (lhen sna) pit of the heart.
dbugs (dbugs) lheb-lheb-tu (lheb lheb tu) ´dug-pa ('dug pa) Pth. *´ug leb leb jhed-de* (*'ug leb leb jhed de*) C. gasping for breath.
now, at present, directly, instantly C.; all (of them) cf. lhams (lhams).
= lhás-ma (lhas ma) 2; also: the act of twisting, plaiting, *hlé-ma gyáb-pa* (*hle ma gyab pa*) C. to twist, to plait.

south, lho-núb (lho nub) south-west, śar-lhó (shar lho) south-east; lhor (lhor), lho-p´yógs-su (lho phyogs su) to the south, towards the south; lhó-k´a (lho kha) prob. = lho (lho); lhó-k´a (lho kha) món-nas (mon nas) ´oṅs ('ongs) Mil. they came from the Mon in the south; lhó-pa (lho pa), lho-´brúg-pa (lho 'brug pa) an inhabitant of Bhotan; lhó-yul (lho yul) acc. to Cunn. the original form of the name of that pro-vince which is now called Lāhul or Lāhōl by the Hindoos, and Lahoul by the English; lho-búr (lho bur) Sch. (also lho-´u-ma (lho 'u ma)), = kug-rna (kug rna).

glóg-pa (glog pa) Cs. a large ulcer or sore, Sch.: carbuncle, anthrax, sbyóṅ-ba (sbyong ba) to cure it; in Med. also nya-lhóg (nya lhog) and gag-lhóg (gag lhog) are mentioned. According to the description, however, which Tibetan phy-sicians gave us of the lhóg-pa (lhog pa), it seems to denote a cancerous ulcer, against which they employ the Aconitum ferox of Nepal, or in default of it some other species of aconite.

Sch. vexation, anger, wrath(?); but: 'lhoṅ (lhong) śor (shor) he has lost the lhoṅ' (lhong), is said of one who was not equal to the exer-tions of incessant meditation, and who in consequence has lost his senses, v. sub smyón-pa (smyon pa).

glod-pa (glod pa), lod-pa (lod pa) or -po (-po), lhod (lhod), lhód-po (lhod po) 1. loose, relaxed, unstrung, slackened, yaṅ-lág (yang lag) of the limbs, e.g. when death approaches S.g., *zúg-po lód-po čá-na* (*zug po lod po cha na*) W. when one gets tired (one cannot help yawning). lhód-pa (lhod pa) sgrím-pa (sgrim pa) to tighten what is loose, lhod lhod (lhod lhod) gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba) to slacken; fig. *´ó-ma lód-po* (*'o ma lod po*) W. the milk begins to fail, milk is scarce. -- 2. of the mind: easy, careless, unconcerned, lhód-de (lhod de) nyol (nyol) cíg (cig) sleep well! sleep soundly! Glr.; blo (blo) lhod (lhod) gyís-la (gyis la) śod (shod) relate the matter calmly, coolly Mil.; śes-pa (shes pa) lhod-cíṅ (lhod cing) in good spirits, of a cheer-ful temper Pth.; ťabs (thabs) śig (shig) yod-kyí (yod kyi) rgyal-po (rgyal po) ťugs (thugs) lhod (lhod) mdzod (mdzod) there is yet a help; there-fore, o king, be of good cheer! Pth.
glón-pa (glon pa) to return, to give or pay back Cs.
